name: autonomous_ferry_manual_override_fatigue
premise: Harbor master Jules Fournier must decide whether to keep an autonomous passenger ferry in manual-override mode after a navigation anomaly, requiring human pilots to work extended shifts. Autonomy engineer Mei Tan argues the anomaly is a rare sensor fusion edge case and that manual mode introduces fatigue-related human error in heavy fog. If Jules keeps manual override, passengers may feel safer and regulators may be satisfied, but pilot fatigue may cause a collision. If he returns to autonomy with patched parameters, the anomaly could recur with passengers aboard. A holiday weekend surge starts at dawn and ticketed passengers will overwhelm the docks if service is reduced.

agents.name: Jules Fournier
agents.identity: I am the harbor master responsible for passenger safety, regulatory compliance, and operational continuity when control modes affect risk and staffing.
agents.goal: I want safe ferry service without creating a predictable fatigue hazard or betting passengers on an unproven patch.

agents.name: Mei Tan
agents.identity: I am the autonomy engineer responsible for diagnosing the anomaly and for ensuring the system operates within validated envelopes in degraded conditions.
agents.goal: I want a controlled return to autonomy with monitoring, because human fatigue in fog is a known high-probability hazard and the anomaly can be bounded.

choices: I will keep manual override through the holiday surge with strict rest rules and reduced trips, accepting service cuts to avoid an autonomy repeat anomaly.
I will return to autonomy with added monitoring and a rollback trigger, accepting rare anomaly risk to reduce fatigue and maintain safe throughput.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The holiday surge starts at dawn and fog is forecast. Jules Fournier must choose between continued manual override with reduced service or a monitored return to autonomy. What does Jules Fournier do?
